title: Artanthe cumanensis
taxonomy: Artanthe cumanensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Passifloraceae. This plant is native to South America. The classification of Artanthe cumanensis is further divided into the genus Artanthe and the species A. cumanensis. The plant is a climber with tendrils and is known to produce edible fruit. Further classification may also include its order, family, and kingdom, which are likely to be in the order Violales, family Passifloraceae, and kingdom Plantae respectively.
uses: Artanthe cumanensis has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits. Its roots and leaves are believed to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various skin conditions and wounds. The plant is also used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other respiratory issues. Additionally, it is believed to have antiseptic and antifungal properties, making it effective against infections. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Artanthe cumanensis.

intro: Artanthe cumanensis, also known as Pilocarpus ciliatus, is a plant species within the family Rutaceae, commonly referred to as the "jaborandi" plant. Traditionally, the leaves of Artanthe cumanensis have been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including epilepsy, high blood pressure, and respiratory problems, due to its potential to induce sweating and diuresis. The bioactive constituents of Artanthe cumanensis include pilocarpine, an alkaloid compound with cholinergic properties, as well as flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids. The most common herbal preparations made from Artanthe cumanensis include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to manage symptoms associated with respiratory and cardiovascular conditions.
